danger_signals.visual_form: Demonic creatures and Zilla Enterprises minions rendered in 3D sprite/voxel forms
danger_signals.meaning: Active threat in environment; players must engage or evade

danger_signals.visual_form: Supernatural beings summoned by Master Zilla with distinct otherworldly appearance
danger_signals.meaning: Ancient supernatural threats; higher danger tier than corporate minions

danger_signals.visual_form: Level design obstacles, traps, and destructible/hazardous objects
danger_signals.meaning: Passive danger from world geometry and puzzle elements

danger_signals.visual_form: Massive samurai-styled mechanical construct piloted by boss antagonist
danger_signals.meaning: Final boss encounter; maximum danger state

safety_signals.visual_form: Inventory items rendered as 3D voxels (not sprites)
safety_signals.meaning: Player vulnerability can be mitigated; safe zones contain supplies

safety_signals.visual_form: Japanese-themed (katana, shurikens) and high-tech weapons (railgun, Uzis) scattered in levels
safety_signals.meaning: Player capability to respond to threats; arsenals in safe/cleared areas

safety_signals.visual_form: Interactable environmental elements with distinct visual markers
safety_signals.meaning: Escape routes and mobility options; tactical advantages available

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Corporate conglomerate iconography (inferred from dominance over major industries)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Corporate minions/enforcers with distinct rendering from supernatural enemies
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Futuristic Japan setting; industrial/corporate architecture associated with Zilla control

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Creatures from the 'dark side' with otherworldly appearance distinct from human minions
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Mystical/supernatural environmental elements; areas where dark forces manifest

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Uses katana and shuriken alongside high-tech weapons; visual bridge between Eastern and Western armaments
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Japanese-themed settings; spaces where player maintains agency

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Futuristic Japan cityscape with corporate architecture; visible industrial dominance over landscape
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Minions patrol and enforce corporate will; NPCs operate under coercion

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Demonic creatures appear throughout levels; supernatural elements manifest in environment
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Creatures attack on sight; hostile entity density increases; environmental corruption visible

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Player-controlled destruction of corporate/supernatural presences; cleared areas show aftermath
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemies respond with increased aggression; Zilla's forces hunt player actively

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Massive samurai war mech appears; concentration of power made physical/visible
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Boss encounter; Zilla pilots mech in direct combat; escape attempt at conclusion

environmental_storytelling.example: Futuristic Japanese corporate environments vs. supernatural/mystical spaces signal thematic conflict between technological dominance and ancient forces
Placement of corporate minions vs. dark side creatures across levels reveals which areas are under which faction control
Japanese-themed weapons (katana, shurikens) mixed with high-tech guns (Uzis, railgun) reflect Lo Wang's dual identity and East-meets-West narrative theme
Presence of drivable tanks and armed vehicles in specific areas suggests military strongholds or strategic locations
Master Zilla's samurai war mech combines Japanese aesthetic with corporate/technological power, visualizing the fusion of tradition and tyranny

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Enforce corporate control; attack when player detected
Pilots massive mech; attempts to kill Lo Wang; escapes upon defeat
Imparts final wisdom to Lo Wang before death; motivation for player's revenge quest
Transitions from content corporate enforcer to active resistance fighter upon discovering Zilla's supernatural conspiracy

notes: Shadow Warrior communicates world-state primarily through enemy type, architectural environment, and weapon/inventory systems. The visual language emphasizes Lo Wang's transition from corporate servant to independent warrior through narrative progression. Faction identity is communicated via aesthetic (corporate/futuristic vs. supernatural/mystical) rather than explicit color coding. The game's humor and Japanese-inspired tone are conveyed through weapon dialogue and character design rather than overt visual systems.
